description:
You are acting as a data-analyst who was just given a small set of transaction records and, before beginning deeper work, you want to capture one quick snapshot of the running system and then calculate the total revenue inside the CSV.  
Perform the following steps entirely from the Linux terminal:

1. A data file already exists at  
   /home/user/data/transactions.csv  
   You do NOT need to create or edit it.  
2. Capture the operating-system name with the command-line utility that prints the kernel/OS name only (hint: the short flag “-s” is usually used).  
3. Calculate the TOTAL revenue in the CSV.  
   • The CSV delimiter is a comma.  
   • The first row is a header.  
   • “quantity” and “price” are columns 3 and 4; revenue for each row is quantity × price.  
4. Write (overwrite if it already exists) a new plain-text file called  
   /home/user/output/analysis_report.log  
   The file MUST contain exactly two lines, in this exact order and format (no extra spaces, no extra lines):  

   Line 1: OS_NAME=<kernel-name>  
   Line 2: TOTAL_REVENUE=<numeric-value-with-two-decimals>  

   Example (the numeric value and kernel name below are only illustrative):  
   OS_NAME=Linux  
   TOTAL_REVENUE=1234.56  

   • <kernel-name> is the literal output you captured in step 2 (case-sensitive).  
   • <numeric-value-with-two-decimals> is the summed revenue rounded and shown with exactly two digits after the decimal point.

You should need no more than two or three simple shell commands to finish this. The automated checker will confirm that /home/user/output/analysis_report.log exists and that its two lines strictly match the required format.
truth:
Initial filesystem state (already present for the agent):

1. Directory: /home/user/data  
2. File: /home/user/data/transactions.csv  
   Exact content (4 lines, '\n' line endings):
   id,product,quantity,price
   1,Widget,3,9.99
   2,Gadget,2,19.50
   3,Doodad,5,4.00

Pre-computed ground-truth values:

• Operating-system name returned by `uname -s` inside the grading container: Linux  
• Correct total revenue:
  (3 × 9.99) + (2 × 19.50) + (5 × 4.00) = 88.97  
  Rounded to two decimal places this is “88.97”.

Required final artefact for the checker:

File: /home/user/output/analysis_report.log  
Exact expected content (2 lines, '\n' line endings, no trailing space):

OS_NAME=Linux
TOTAL_REVENUE=88.97
name: task_000000_911a5c92
